tldr: Tier-1 schema work is now shipped: Physician, per-service MedicalTherapy, FAQPage per service, BreadcrumbList + visible breadcrumbs, AggregateRating + Reviews, dateModified + visible 'Last updated', sameAs, and the homepage H1 / expanded llms.txt all landed this week. Nine punch-list items closed.

First search-visibility baseline: Meridian is not in the Google top-10 for ANY of the 25 tracked queries. Competitors dominating osteopathy: betterhealthosteopathy.nz, christchurchosteopath.co.nz, osteo.co.nz, osteopath.nz, mkosteopathy.co.nz, osteopath-christchurch.com. Acupuncture: thehouseofacupuncture.co.nz owns the cluster. Dry-needling queries are owned entirely by physios (musclepeople.co.nz, fixedphysio.co.nz) — exactly the gap item #17 was created to close.

Biggest remaining gaps are content, not schema: no condition pages (item #10 — sciatica/headaches/pregnancy/sports still in backlog), no neck-pain or back-pain page (new items #22, #23), no fertility-acupuncture landing (new item #24), and none of last week's ChatGPT-surfaced fixes (#17 acupuncture reframe, #18 evidence-informed language, #19 suburb signals, #20 'Treats:' tags, #21 GBP services) have shipped yet — each addresses a concrete SERP gap confirmed by this week's visibility checks.
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
FAQPage JSON-LD on the homepage
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page
Auto-generated sitemap with lastmod
AggregateRating + Review schema (ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 65, three real testimonials) nested under MedicalBusiness
Physician JSON-LD per team profile with sameAs to professional registers and visible 'Registered with …' block
Per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema with provider, areaServed, hasOfferCatalog of specialties, and mainEntityOfPage
FAQPage JSON-LD on every service page (driven by faq arrays in services.json)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + visible breadcrumb strip on non-home pages
Visible 'Last updated' block on service and team pages, with dateModified in WebPage schema
sameAs block on clinic JSON-LD (Google Business Profile etc.)
Expanded llms.txt — Conditions-treated, Pricing-&-ACC, Common-questions sections
Homepage H1 reframed to 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch'; practitioners section demoted to H2

/conditions/sciatica/, /conditions/headaches-migraines/, /conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/, /conditions/sports-injury-recovery/ — four condition Q→A pages with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, plus a What We Treat nav dropdown
ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage @graph schema on every team profile (alongside the Physician node), so each practitioner URL is its own retrievable AI-search entity
Hero subtitle and practitioners intro reframed to evidence-informed language (no longer says "holistic"); each line is now distinct rather than restating the H1
"Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs" — site-wide footer block + extended llms.txt Area-served line
Treats: chips on every team profile (in a What I Treat card), with Nina's row carrying a clickable pink-tinted pill linking to the new fertility page
/services/acupuncture/fertility/ sub-page — MedicalTherapy schema linking to the parent acupuncture #service, FAQPage on IVF timing / ACC status / Nina's credentials, hero + 3 specialty images

gaps.title: No condition-specific pages
gaps.why: No indexable URL for 'sciatica osteopath Christchurch' etc. — biggest content gap, confirmed this week by zero top-10 presence on all condition-led queries.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months; osteopath-christchurch.com is winning headache/migraine queries purely on blog-post velocity.

gaps.title: No MedicalWebPage / ProfilePage per team profile
gaps.why: Physician schema is now per-profile, but each URL should also be a standalone retrievable ProfilePage node so AI engines can cite the individual page.

gaps.title: No llms.txt reference in HTML head
gaps.why: Emerging convention; several indexers check for a <link rel='llms'> or similar in the head.

gaps.title: Image alt text could be richer
gaps.why: AI-image-grounded models benefit from descriptive, not titular, alt text.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Reddit visibility now accounts for 37% of AI-Overview citations from social/forum sources — industry signal from April 2026.

gaps.title: Acupuncture page leads with TCM framing
gaps.why: Confirmed by SERP: physios own every 'dry needling' slot because our acupuncture page leads with 'Traditional Chinese Medicine'.

gaps.title: 'Holistic' language downgrades positioning
gaps.why: ChatGPT labelled Meridian 'holistic but still evidence-based' — the 'but' subtly downgrades vs competitors framed as 'science-based / structured'.

gaps.title: No catchment-suburb signals for local queries
gaps.why: Halswell, Addington, Sydenham, Wigram — zero mentions on our site; suburb-branded clinics (Halswell Rd Clinic, osteo.co.nz/halswell) outrank us for their own name.

gaps.title: Practitioner profiles lead with a single specialty
gaps.why: AI engines cite the LEADING specialty; Nina's 'women's health' lead means she doesn't surface for neck/headache queries.

gaps.title: Google Business Profile services incomplete
gaps.why: ChatGPT pulls clinic names + service offerings directly from GBP. Missing GBP services = missed AI citations.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Feeds the freshness signal AI engines reward.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. April-2026 industry data: Reddit is now 37% of AI-Overview citations from social/forum sources.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old.

items.title: Reframe acupuncture page as Western medical / dry needling first
items.body: Surfaced by a real-world ChatGPT test: the user searched 'Western acupuncture / dry needling near Halswell' and ChatGPT routed them to physio clinics first because our acupuncture page leads with 'Traditional Chinese Medicine with modern evidence-based practice'. Flip the ordering: lead with 'Medical acupuncture / dry needling — Western evidence-based technique for trigger-point and musculoskeletal pain', then mention TCM as a complementary framework. Name Nina's Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) in the first paragraph — it's the exact credential ChatGPT told users to look for. 2026-04-24 confirmed: every top-10 slot for 'dry needling christchurch' is a physio clinic.

items.title: Audit and expand Google Business Profile services
items.body: ChatGPT pulled the clinic as 'Meridian Osteopathy - Medical Acupuncture Clinic' straight from GBP. Whatever services are listed on GBP becomes what AI tools can cite. Log into Google Business Profile and add these services: Osteopathy, Medical acupuncture, Dry needling, Neck pain treatment, Headache & migraine treatment, Sports injury treatment, Pregnancy back pain, Paediatric osteopathy, Herbal medicine. ~10 minutes of clicking. Cannot be shipped by the Ship-it button — owner task.

items.title: Not ranking for 'neck pain osteopath christchurch' — betterhealthosteopathy.nz owns it
items.body: betterhealthosteopathy.nz and osteopath.nz both have dedicated neck-pain condition URLs that win this query; Meridian is not in the top 10. This is NOT covered by item #10's four-page scope (sciatica / headaches / pregnancy / sports). Add a 5th condition page — 800–1200 words Q→A with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema — mirroring the item #10 pattern. Internal-link from the osteopathy service page.

items.title: Not ranking for 'back pain osteopath christchurch' — betterhealthosteopathy.nz owns it
items.body: Better Health's /back-pain-relief-osteopath-christchurch/ is the #1 result for this query cluster. Back pain is probably the single highest-intent query osteopathy patients search — it must have its own page. Mirror the item #10 pattern: MedicalCondition + FAQPage, cross-link to ACC info and the osteopathy service page, include a section on acute vs chronic and when to seek care.

items.title: Not ranking for 'osteopath acc christchurch' — osteo.co.nz owns it
items.body: osteo.co.nz has a dedicated ACC landing page that wins this query. We already have the ACC pricing row in services.json and scattered ACC mentions, but no single indexable /acc/ URL. Build a short page (~500 words) explaining ACC-funded treatment at Meridian, the current ACC rate, no referral required, how to lodge a claim, and link out to both service pages and a booking CTA. Low-effort content fix.
